judgmentEn: Biting Through has success. It is favorable to let justice be administered.
imageZh: 雷电噬嗑；先王以明罚敕法。
imageEn: Thunder and lightning: the image of Biting Through. Thus the kings of former times made firm the laws through clearly defined penalties.
interpretationZh: 噬嗑卦象征咬合，口中有物阻隔，须咬断方能合拢。雷电交加，刚柔并济，象征以刑罚明法度，去除障碍。
interpretationEn: Shi He symbolizes biting through an obstacle. Thunder and lightning combine force and clarity, representing the use of law and penalties to remove obstructions.

lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: His feet are fastened in the stocks, so that his toes disappear. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 脚戴刑具遮住脚趾，小惩大诫，及时制止过错则无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Feet in the stocks hide the toes. A small punishment prevents greater wrongdoing. No blame.
lines.detailZh: 初九阳刚居噬嗑之初，象征初犯小过。"屦校灭趾"描绘脚上戴着木枷、遮住了脚趾——这是最轻的刑罚，相当于现代的警告处分。"无咎"表示虽然受了小惩罚，但因为及时纠正，不会有大的过失。初九的智慧在于"惩小恶以戒大恶"——小错误及时惩戒，才能防止发展成大问题。教育孩子、管理团队都是同样的道理：发现苗头就要及时纠正。
lines.detailEn: The first nine, a yang line at the beginning of Shi He, symbolizes a first-time minor offense. 'Feet in stocks so that the toes disappear' depicts wearing wooden shackles on the feet that cover the toes—the lightest punishment, equivalent to a modern warning or reprimand. 'No blame' indicates that though receiving minor punishment, timely correction prevents major fault. The wisdom of the first nine lies in 'punishing small wrongs to prevent great ones'—promptly disciplining minor errors prevents them from developing into major problems. The same principle applies to raising children and managing teams: correct problems at the first sign.

lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Bites through tender meat, so that his nose disappears. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 咬入柔软的肉中，鼻子都没入其中，虽用刑过重但无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Biting through soft meat so deeply the nose is covered. Firm action, though strong, brings no blame.
lines.detailZh: 六二阴柔居中得正，象征执法者面对普通案件。"噬肤灭鼻"描绘咬入柔软的肉（肤）直到鼻子都埋进去——案件不太困难，轻松就能处理。"无咎"表示处理得当，没有过失。六二居中位有中庸之德，执法不偏不倚、恰到好处。"肤"是柔软的肉，象征案件本身不复杂，犯罪者也不是什么大人物。六二告诉我们：大多数案件其实并不复杂，只要秉公执法、不偏不倚，就能顺利解决。
lines.detailEn: The second six, a yin line achieving centrality and correctness, symbolizes a law enforcer handling an ordinary case. 'Biting through tender meat so that the nose is covered' depicts biting into soft flesh so deeply that the nose is buried—the case is not difficult and can be handled with ease. 'No blame' indicates proper handling without fault. The second six, in the central position with the virtue of the mean, enforces law impartially and appropriately. 'Tender meat' symbolizes a straightforward case where the offender is no powerful figure. The second six teaches us: most cases are actually not complex—as long as one enforces the law fairly and impartially, they can be resolved smoothly.

lines.textZh: 六三：噬腊肉，遇毒；小吝，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Bites on old dried meat and strikes on something poisonous. Slight humiliation. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 咬干腊肉遇到毒物，虽有小困窘但无大过。
lines.interpretationEn: Biting on dried meat and encountering poison. Slight humiliation but no blame.
lines.detailZh: 六三阴柔不中不正，象征执法者遇到棘手案件。"噬腊肉遇毒"描绘咬老腊肉时遇到了毒素——案件看似普通，实际暗藏危险。"小吝无咎"表示虽然会有些困难和不顺，但最终不会有大过失。六三的位置尴尬，能力不足以应对复杂局面，但只要坚持原则、不被"毒素"（贿赂、威胁、人情）所侵蚀，就能化险为夷。这提醒执法者：越是棘手的案件，越要警惕暗中的陷阱。
lines.detailEn: The third six, a yin line achieving neither centrality nor correctness, symbolizes a law enforcer encountering a thorny case. 'Biting on old dried meat and encountering poison' depicts biting into aged jerky only to encounter toxins—the case appears ordinary but actually harbors hidden dangers. 'A little humiliation, but no blame' indicates some difficulty and setbacks but ultimately no major fault. The third six's awkward position means its capability is insufficient for complex situations, but as long as one adheres to principles and is not corrupted by 'poison' (bribes, threats, personal favors), danger can be averted. This reminds law enforcers: the more difficult the case, the more vigilant one must be against hidden traps.

lines.textZh: 九四：噬干胏，得金矢，利艰贞，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Bites on dried gristly meat. Receives metal arrows. It furthers one to be mindful of difficulties and to be persevering. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 咬带骨的干肉，得到金属箭矢，利于在艰难中坚守正道，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Biting on dried gristly meat and finding metal arrows. Persevere through difficulty for good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 九四阳刚居上卦之初，象征执法者面对重大案件。"噬干胏得金矢"描绘咬带骨头的干肉时发现了金箭头——案件非常困难（干肉带骨），但坚持到底会有意外收获（金矢）。"利艰贞吉"告诫要做好打硬仗的准备，坚守正道才能获吉。"金矢"象征刚直和正义，也暗示在艰难的执法过程中会发现重要线索或获得关键证据。九四鼓励执法者：越是困难的案件，越可能有重大突破。
lines.detailEn: The fourth nine, a yang line at the base of the upper trigram, symbolizes a law enforcer facing a major case. 'Biting on dried gristly meat and finding a metal arrowhead' depicts biting into tough dried meat with bone and discovering a metal arrowhead—the case is extremely difficult (dried meat with bone) but perseverance yields unexpected rewards (the arrowhead). 'It is favorable to be mindful of difficulties and to be persevering. Good fortune' counsels preparing for a tough battle; only by maintaining the right path can one achieve good fortune. The 'metal arrowhead' symbolizes uprightness and justice, also suggesting that during difficult enforcement, important clues or key evidence may be discovered. The fourth nine encourages law enforcers: the more difficult the case, the greater the potential for a major breakthrough.

lines.textZh: 六五：噬干肉，得黄金，贞厉，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Bites on dried lean meat. Receives yellow gold. Perseveringly aware of danger. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 咬干肉得到黄金，坚守正道并警惕危险，则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Biting on dried meat and receiving yellow gold. Remain aware of danger. No blame.
lines.detailZh: 六五阴柔居君位，是噬嗑卦的最高裁判者。"噬干肉得黄金"描绘咬干肉时发现了黄金——执法虽然辛苦，但公正的裁决会带来丰厚的回报。"贞厉无咎"表示坚守正道虽有危险但无过失。六五以阴柔居尊位，象征以仁慈之心行使刑罚之权——这是最理想的司法态度。"黄金"的"黄"是中色，象征中庸；"金"象征刚正。六五告诉我们：最好的法官既有仁慈的心肠，又有刚正的原则。
lines.detailEn: The fifth six, a yin line in the ruler's position, is Shi He's supreme adjudicator. 'Biting on dried lean meat and finding yellow gold' depicts discovering gold while biting through dried meat—though enforcement is arduous, just verdicts bring rich rewards. 'Perseverance in awareness of danger, no blame' indicates that maintaining the right path despite danger incurs no fault. The fifth six, yielding in the honored position, symbolizes exercising punitive authority with a compassionate heart—the ideal judicial temperament. In 'yellow gold,' 'yellow' is the color of the center, symbolizing the mean; 'gold' symbolizes uprightness. The fifth six teaches us: the finest judge possesses both a compassionate heart and principled integrity.

lines.textEn: Nine at the top: His neck is fastened in the wooden cangue, so that his ears disappear. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 颈戴枷锁遮住耳朵，刑罚至极，积恶不改终致凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: Neck in the cangue so the ears disappear. Punishment reaches its extreme. Misfortune.
lines.detailZh: 上九阳刚居噬嗑之极，象征罪大恶极、不可救药的罪犯。"何校灭耳"描绘脖子上戴着沉重的木枷、遮住了耳朵——这是最重的刑罚，与初九的"屦校灭趾"形成首尾对比。"凶"字直接宣判了结局。上九的悲剧在于：从初九的小过到上九的大罪，是一步步积累的结果。如果在初九阶段就及时纠正，何至于走到上九的绝境？上九是对所有人的终极警告：不要忽视小错误，积小恶终成大恶。
lines.detailEn: The top nine, a yang line at the extreme of Shi He, symbolizes an incorrigible criminal of the gravest offenses. 'Wearing a wooden cangue so that the ears disappear' depicts a heavy wooden collar around the neck covering the ears—the most severe punishment, forming a stark contrast with the first nine's 'feet in stocks.' 'Misfortune' directly pronounces the outcome. The tragedy of the top nine lies in this: from the first nine's minor offense to the top nine's grave crime, the progression was gradual accumulation. Had correction come at the first nine's stage, how could things have reached the top nine's desperate end? The top nine serves as the ultimate warning to all: never ignore small mistakes—accumulated minor wrongs inevitably become great crimes.

overviewZh: 噬嗑卦是《周易》第二十一卦，由震下离上组成，雷电交加，象征天威赫赫、刑罚严明。"噬嗑"意为咬合——口中有物阻隔上下颚，必须用力咬碎才能合拢，比喻用刑罚清除障碍、恢复秩序。噬嗑卦是观卦之后的自然发展——观察到问题之后，就要采取行动解决。《序卦传》云："可观而后有所合，故受之以噬嗑。"噬嗑卦的核心智慧在于：当障碍无法通过柔和手段消除时，就必须果断使用强制力量。但刑罚的目的不是惩罚本身，而是恢复和谐。
overviewEn: Shi He (Biting Through) is the twenty-first hexagram of the I Ching, composed of Thunder (Zhen) below and Fire (Li) above. Thunder and lightning together symbolize awesome celestial power and strict enforcement of law. 'Shi He' means biting through—something obstructs the mouth between the upper and lower jaws, which must be forcefully bitten through to close, metaphorically representing the use of punishment to remove obstacles and restore order. Shi He naturally follows Guan (Contemplation)—after observing problems, one must take action to resolve them. The Sequence of Hexagrams states: 'When there is something to contemplate, union will follow, hence Shi He follows.' The core wisdom of Shi He lies in understanding that when obstacles cannot be removed through gentle means, decisive force must be employed. But the purpose of punishment is not punishment itself—it is the restoration of harmony.
judgmentDetailZh: 噬嗑卦辞"亨，利用狱"简洁明了。"亨"表示通过咬合（刑罚）可以达到亨通——障碍被清除，上下才能合拢，事情才能顺利推进。"利用狱"直接指出适宜使用刑罚和司法手段。这在六十四卦中非常罕见，大多数卦都强调德治和柔道，唯独噬嗑卦明确肯定了法治的必要性。但"利用狱"不是鼓励滥刑，而是在特定情况下（有障碍阻隔时）的必要手段。正如医生切除肿瘤是为了救人，刑罚的目的是为了恢复社会的健康运转。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment of Shi He—'Success. It is favorable to let justice be administered'—is concise and direct. 'Success' indicates that through biting through (punishment), prosperity can be achieved—only when obstacles are removed can upper and lower unite and affairs proceed smoothly. 'Favorable to let justice be administered' directly states that employing punishment and judicial measures is appropriate. This is extremely rare among the sixty-four hexagrams; most emphasize virtuous governance and gentle methods, yet Shi He explicitly affirms the necessity of rule by law. However, 'favorable for justice' does not encourage excessive punishment but represents a necessary measure under specific circumstances (when obstacles block the way). Just as a surgeon removes a tumor to save a life, the purpose of punishment is to restore society's healthy functioning.
imageDetailZh: 象辞"雷电噬嗑，先王以明罚敕法"将自然威力与法治精神完美结合。"雷电"同时出现，雷声震慑、电光照明——雷代表刑罚的威严，电代表法律的明察。先王效法这一天象，做两件事："明罚"是让刑罚公开透明，让所有人都知道什么行为会受到什么惩罚；"敕法"是整饬法律制度，使法令严明、执行有力。"明"字是关键——刑罚必须公开、公正、公平，暗箱操作的刑罚不是正义而是暴政。雷电的特点是瞬间爆发、光明正大，这正是理想司法的特征。
imageDetailEn: The Image text—'Thunder and lightning: the image of Biting Through. Thus the kings of former times made firm the laws through clearly defined penalties'—perfectly unites natural power with the spirit of rule by law. Thunder and lightning appear simultaneously—thunder intimidates while lightning illuminates. Thunder represents the majesty of punishment; lightning represents the clarity of law. The ancient kings emulated this pattern with two actions: 'clearly defined penalties' means making punishments transparent and public, ensuring everyone knows what consequences follow what actions; 'making firm the laws' means rectifying the legal system to ensure laws are strict and enforcement is vigorous. The word 'clearly' is crucial—punishment must be open, just, and fair; punishment conducted in secret is not justice but tyranny. The nature of thunder and lightning is instantaneous eruption in full openness—precisely the characteristics of ideal justice.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】噬嗑卦适用于企业整顿和制度建设。当团队中出现严重违规行为时，不能姑息迁就，必须"利用狱"——严格按制度处理。初九"屦校灭趾"提醒小错要及时纠正，防微杜渐。上九"何校灭耳"警告如果放任不管，最终会酿成大祸。六五"噬干肉得黄金"表示公正执法会获得意外收获。【感情】噬嗑卦提醒感情中不能回避矛盾。当两人之间有"障碍物"（误解、第三者、原则分歧）时，必须正面解决，不能装作看不见。【财运】噬嗑卦暗示投资中要果断止损，对不良资产要"咬碎"清除。六三"噬腊肉遇毒"警告有些看似有利可图的投资实际暗藏风险。【健康】雷电象征强烈的能量释放，提醒注意心血管健康和情绪管理。牙齿和消化系统也是噬嗑卦关注的重点。
modernApplicationEn: In career, Shi He applies to corporate rectification and institutional building. When serious violations occur within a team, they cannot be tolerated—one must 'let justice be administered' and handle matters strictly according to policy. The first line's 'feet in stocks' reminds us to correct small errors promptly, nipping problems in the bud. The top line's 'neck in a wooden cangue' warns that unchecked problems eventually become catastrophic. The fifth line's 'biting dried meat, finding gold' indicates that fair enforcement yields unexpected rewards. In relationships, Shi He reminds us not to avoid conflicts. When 'obstacles' exist between two people (misunderstandings, third parties, fundamental disagreements), they must be confronted directly rather than ignored. In finance, Shi He suggests decisively cutting losses and 'biting through' bad assets to eliminate them. The third line's 'biting dried meat, encountering poison' warns that some seemingly profitable investments actually harbor hidden risks. In health, thunder and lightning symbolize intense energy release, reminding us to attend to cardiovascular health and emotional management. Teeth and the digestive system are also focal points of Shi He.
historicalStoryZh: 噬嗑卦最著名的典故是包拯断案。包拯以铁面无私著称，不畏权贵、秉公执法，正是"雷电噬嗑"的人间化身。他审理的每一个案件都做到"明罚敕法"——公开审理、依法判决，让百姓信服。另一个典故是商鞅"徙木立信"后的严格执法。商鞅变法初期，太子犯法，商鞅毫不留情地处罚了太子的老师（因太子年幼不能受刑），以此向全国宣示法律面前人人平等。虽然商鞅最终因得罪权贵而被车裂，但他建立的法治精神成为秦国统一天下的基石。
historicalStoryEn: The most celebrated historical story associated with Shi He is Judge Bao Zheng's adjudication. Bao Zheng was renowned for his iron impartiality, fearless before the powerful and strictly upholding the law—a human embodiment of 'thunder and lightning biting through.' Every case he tried achieved 'clearly defined penalties and firm laws'—open trials, lawful verdicts, earning the people's trust. Another famous story is Shang Yang's strict enforcement after 'moving the wooden pole.' Early in his reforms, when the Crown Prince violated the law, Shang Yang unhesitatingly punished the Prince's tutor (since the Prince was too young for punishment), declaring to the entire nation that all are equal before the law. Though Shang Yang was ultimately torn apart by chariots for offending the nobility, the spirit of rule by law he established became the cornerstone of Qin's unification of the realm.
relatedHexagramsNote: 噬嗑卦的错卦为井卦（第48卦），刑罚与养民互为表里；综卦为贲卦（第22卦），噬嗑主刑罚之实，贲主文饰之美，刚柔相济；互卦为蹇卦（第39卦），提醒执法过程中会遇到重重困难。

judgmentEn: Grace has success. In small matters it is favorable to undertake something.
imageZh: 山下有火，贲；君子以明庶政，无敢折狱。
imageEn: Fire at the foot of the mountain: the image of Grace. Thus does the superior man proceed when clearing up current affairs. But he dare not decide controversial issues in this way.
interpretationZh: 贲卦象征文饰，山下有火，光彩照耀。文饰虽美但不可过度，小事可行，大事不宜仅凭外表。
interpretationEn: Bi symbolizes grace and adornment. Fire beneath the mountain illuminates with beauty. Ornament aids small matters, but substance must underlie great decisions.

lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: He lends grace to his toes, leaves the carriage, and walks.
lines.interpretationZh: 装饰脚趾，舍弃车辆步行，安于朴素，不慕虚荣。
lines.interpretationEn: Adorning the toes, he leaves the carriage and walks. He chooses simplicity over vanity.
lines.detailZh: 初九阳刚居贲卦之初，象征装饰从最基础的地方开始。"贲其趾"是装饰脚趾——从最低处、最基础的地方开始美化。"舍车而徒"表示放弃乘车而选择步行——宁可朴素地走路，也不要华而不实地坐车。初九的智慧在于：真正的美从脚踏实地开始，不需要借助外在的排场。一个人的品格修养要从最基本的言行举止做起，而不是追求表面的光鲜。
lines.detailEn: The first nine, a yang line at the beginning of Bi, symbolizes adornment starting from the most fundamental place. 'Gracing the toes' means adorning the toes—beautifying from the lowest, most basic point. 'Leaving the carriage and going on foot' means abandoning the carriage to walk—preferring to walk simply rather than ride in empty splendor. The wisdom of the first nine: true beauty begins with keeping one's feet on the ground, requiring no external pomp. A person's character cultivation must start from the most basic words and actions rather than pursuing superficial glamour.

lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Lends grace to the beard on his chin.
lines.interpretationZh: 装饰胡须，依附于上，随上而动。
lines.interpretationEn: Adorning the beard. Grace follows the higher principle, moving with what is above.
lines.detailZh: 六二阴柔居中得正，与九三比邻。"贲其须"是装饰胡须——胡须依附于下巴，不能独立存在。六二的装饰是依附性的，它的美来自于与九三的关系，而非自身独立的光彩。这提醒我们：有些美是依附性的，离开了依附对象就失去了意义。在职场中，有些人的价值完全依赖于某个领导或平台，一旦离开就黯然失色。六二告诫：要培养独立的价值，不要做"须"一样的附属品。
lines.detailEn: The second six, a yin line achieving centrality and correctness, is adjacent to the third nine. 'Gracing the beard' means adorning the beard—a beard depends on the chin and cannot exist independently. The second six's adornment is dependent; its beauty derives from its relationship with the third nine rather than its own independent radiance. This reminds us: some beauty is dependent, losing meaning when separated from its object of attachment. In the workplace, some people's value depends entirely on a particular leader or platform, fading once removed. The second six warns: cultivate independent value rather than being an accessory like a 'beard.'

lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: Graceful and moist. Constant perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 文饰润泽，光彩照人，长久坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Graceful and glistening. Lasting perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 九三阳刚居下卦之上，是下卦离火的最上一爻，光彩最为绚烂。"贲如濡如"形容装饰得光彩照人、润泽华美。"永贞吉"告诫要永远坚守正道才能吉利。九三处于文饰最盛的位置，容易沉迷于外在的华丽而忘记内在的修养。"永贞"二字是关键提醒：越是光彩照人的时候，越要保持内心的坚定和正直。否则就会变成金玉其外、败絮其中。
lines.detailEn: The third nine, a yang line atop the lower trigram, is the uppermost line of the lower Li (Fire) trigram, where brilliance is most resplendent. 'Graceful and moist' describes being adorned with dazzling radiance and lustrous beauty. 'Eternal perseverance brings good fortune' counsels that only by forever maintaining the right path can one achieve good fortune. The third nine occupies the position of peak adornment, easily becoming intoxicated by external splendor while forgetting inner cultivation. 'Eternal perseverance' is the crucial reminder: the more dazzling one appears, the more one must maintain inner steadfastness and integrity. Otherwise one becomes gold and jade on the outside, cotton waste within.

lines.textZh: 六四：贲如皤如，白马翰如，匪寇婚媾。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Grace or simplicity? A white horse comes as if on wings. He is not a robber; he will woo at the right time.
lines.interpretationZh: 朴素如白，白马飞驰而来，来者非寇而是求婚者。
lines.interpretationEn: Grace or plainness? A white winged horse approaches. Not a robber but a wooer.
lines.detailZh: 六四阴柔居上卦之初，处于文饰由盛转衰的转折点。"贲如皤如，白马翰如"描绘一幅朴素而动人的画面——素白的装扮、白马飞驰而来。"匪寇婚媾"表示来者不是强盗而是求婚者。六四放弃了华丽的装饰，回归朴素真诚，反而更加动人。这就像求婚时，真诚的表白比奢华的排场更能打动人心。六四标志着贲卦从追求华丽到回归朴素的重要转变。
lines.detailEn: The fourth six, a yin line at the base of the upper trigram, stands at the turning point where adornment shifts from flourishing to fading. 'Grace or simplicity? A white horse comes as if on wings' paints a simple yet moving picture—plain white attire, a white horse galloping forward. 'Not a robber but a wooer' indicates the arrival is not a bandit but a suitor. The fourth six abandons elaborate decoration, returning to plain sincerity, and becomes more moving as a result. Like a marriage proposal where a sincere declaration touches the heart more than lavish spectacle. The fourth six marks Bi's important transition from pursuing splendor to returning to simplicity.

lines.textZh: 六五：贲于丘园，束帛戋戋，吝，终吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Grace in hills and gardens. The roll of silk is meager and small. Humiliation, but in the end good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 在丘园中修饰，礼物虽薄但出于真心，虽有困窘终获吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Grace in hills and gardens. The silk offering is small but sincere. Initial humiliation, but good fortune in the end.
lines.detailZh: 六五阴柔居君位，象征最高统治者的审美取向。"贲于丘园"是在山丘和园林中寻找装饰之美——不是金碧辉煌的宫殿，而是自然朴素的山水。"束帛戋戋"形容礼物虽然简薄（一束丝帛），但心意真诚。"吝终吉"表示虽然开始时显得寒酸，但最终结果是吉利的。六五的审美境界已经超越了物质的华丽，回归到自然的朴素之美。这是"大巧若拙、大美若朴"的体现。
lines.detailEn: The fifth six, a yin line in the ruler's position, symbolizes the supreme ruler's aesthetic orientation. 'Grace in hills and gardens' means seeking beauty in hills and gardens—not in gilded palaces but in nature's simplicity. 'A roll of silk, small and meager' describes a gift that is modest (a bundle of silk) but sincere in intention. 'Humiliation, but in the end good fortune' indicates that though initially appearing meager, the final outcome is auspicious. The fifth six's aesthetic sensibility has transcended material splendor, returning to nature's simple beauty. This embodies 'great skill appears clumsy, great beauty appears plain.'

lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Simple grace. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 朴素无华的装饰，返璞归真，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Simple, unadorned grace. Return to the essential. No blame.
lines.detailZh: 上九阳刚居贲卦之极，是文饰之道的最高境界。"白贲，无咎"——白色的装饰，没有过失。白色是一切颜色的基础，也是最朴素的颜色。到了贲卦的极致，最高级的装饰反而是不装饰——回归本色、返璞归真。这与老子"大音希声、大象无形"的思想相通。上九告诉我们：真正的美不需要刻意雕琢，最高级的文明是自然而然的。从初九的"贲其趾"到上九的"白贲"，完成了从追求装饰到超越装饰的完整历程。
lines.detailEn: The top nine, a yang line at the extreme of Bi, represents the highest realm of adornment. 'Simple grace, no blame'—white adornment, without fault. White is the foundation of all colors and the most unadorned color. At Bi's pinnacle, the highest adornment is paradoxically non-adornment—returning to one's original nature, reverting to the uncarved block. This resonates with Laozi's thought: 'Great music has the faintest notes; the great form is without shape.' The top nine teaches us: true beauty requires no deliberate crafting; the highest civilization is natural and spontaneous. From the first nine's 'gracing the toes' to the top nine's 'simple grace,' the complete journey from pursuing adornment to transcending adornment is fulfilled.

overviewZh: 贲卦是《周易》第二十二卦，由离下艮上组成，山下有火，象征火光照耀山体，使山呈现出绚丽的色彩。"贲"字本义为装饰、文饰，引申为文明、文化之美。贲卦是噬嗑卦的综卦——噬嗑主刑罚之实，贲主文饰之美，刚柔相济。《序卦传》云："物不可以苟合而已，故受之以贲。贲者饰也。"事物不能只是粗糙地合在一起，还需要文饰修饰。贲卦的核心智慧在于：形式美和内容美同样重要，但文饰不能过度，要以质朴为本。
overviewEn: Bi (Grace / Adornment) is the twenty-second hexagram of the I Ching, composed of Fire (Li) below and Mountain (Gen) above. Fire beneath the mountain symbolizes firelight illuminating the mountainside, revealing brilliant colors. The character 'Bi' originally means to adorn or embellish, extended to mean civilization and cultural beauty. Bi is the inverse hexagram of Shi He (Biting Through)—Shi He governs the substance of punishment while Bi governs the beauty of adornment, combining firmness with grace. The Sequence of Hexagrams states: 'Things cannot merely be thrown together carelessly, hence Bi follows. Bi means adornment.' Things cannot simply be crudely combined—they require refinement and embellishment. The core wisdom of Bi lies in understanding that formal beauty and substantive beauty are equally important, but adornment must not become excessive—simplicity must remain the foundation.
judgmentDetailZh: 贲卦卦辞"亨，小利有攸往"透露出微妙的信息。"亨"表示文饰之道可以亨通，但只是"小利有攸往"——小有利于前行，而非大利。这暗示文饰虽然重要，但毕竟是"小道"，不能与实质性的大道相提并论。过度追求形式美而忽视内容，就像金玉其外败絮其中。贲卦的"小利"提醒我们：适度的文饰能锦上添花，但不能喧宾夺主。真正的美是内外兼修——既有实质的内涵，又有得体的外表。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment of Bi—'Success. In small matters it is favorable to undertake something'—conveys a subtle message. 'Success' indicates that the way of adornment can prosper, but only 'in small matters is it favorable'—slightly favorable for proceeding, not greatly so. This implies that while adornment is important, it remains a 'minor way' that cannot be equated with the substantive great Way. Excessive pursuit of formal beauty while neglecting content is like being gold and jade on the outside but cotton waste within. Bi's 'small favor' reminds us: moderate adornment enhances beauty, but it must not overshadow substance. True beauty cultivates both interior and exterior—possessing genuine substance along with appropriate appearance.
imageDetailZh: 象辞"山下有火，贲；君子以明庶政，无敢折狱"将自然美景与治理智慧相结合。"山下有火"描绘火光映照山体的美丽景象——火光虽美，但只能照亮山的表面，无法穿透山体。君子效法这一天象，"明庶政"是用文饰之道来处理日常政务，使政令优美得体；"无敢折狱"是不敢轻率地判决诉讼案件——因为文饰只能处理表面问题，涉及实质性的司法判决则需要更深入的洞察。这与噬嗑卦的"利用狱"形成鲜明对比：噬嗑可以断狱，贲则不可，因为文饰之道不适合处理需要实质判断的事务。
imageDetailEn: The Image text—'Fire at the foot of the mountain: the image of Grace. Thus does the superior man proceed when clearing up current affairs, but he dare not decide controversial issues in this way'—unites natural beauty with governance wisdom. 'Fire at the foot of the mountain' depicts the beautiful scene of firelight illuminating the mountainside—though the fire's light is beautiful, it can only illuminate the mountain's surface without penetrating its interior. The superior man emulates this pattern: 'clearing up current affairs' means using the way of adornment to handle routine governance, making decrees elegant and appropriate; 'daring not decide controversial issues' means not rashly adjudicating legal cases—because adornment can only address surface matters, while substantive judicial decisions require deeper insight. This forms a sharp contrast with Shi He's 'favorable for justice': Shi He can adjudicate; Bi cannot, because the way of adornment is unsuitable for matters requiring substantive judgment.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】贲卦强调"包装"和"品牌"的重要性。好的产品需要好的包装，但包装不能替代产品本身的质量。初九"贲其趾"提醒从基础做起，脚踏实地。六五"贲于丘园"建议回归朴素，在简约中寻找真正的美。上九"白贲"揭示最高级的装饰就是不装饰——大道至简。【感情】贲卦提醒感情中外在形式（浪漫仪式、礼物）和内在真情同样重要。六二"贲其须"象征依附于他人的装饰，提醒不要为了取悦对方而失去自我。六四"贲如皤如白马翰如"描绘朴素真诚的求婚，比华丽的排场更动人。【财运】贲卦暗示注重品牌价值和产品设计的投资方向。但"小利有攸往"提醒收益有限，不宜重仓。上九"白贲无咎"建议投资回归价值本质，不追逐华而不实的概念股。【健康】山下有火象征内热，注意上火和炎症。贲卦也提醒注重外在形象管理，适度的仪容整理有助于心理健康。
modernApplicationEn: In career, Bi emphasizes the importance of 'packaging' and 'branding.' Good products need good packaging, but packaging cannot substitute for product quality itself. The first line's 'gracing the toes' reminds us to start from the foundation, keeping feet on the ground. The fifth line's 'grace in hills and gardens' suggests returning to simplicity, finding true beauty in minimalism. The top line's 'simple grace' reveals that the highest adornment is no adornment—the great Way is simple. In relationships, Bi reminds us that external forms (romantic rituals, gifts) and inner sincerity are equally important. The second line's 'gracing the beard' symbolizes adornment dependent on others, warning against losing oneself to please a partner. The fourth line's 'grace or simplicity, a white horse comes flying' depicts a plain, sincere courtship more moving than lavish display. In finance, Bi suggests investment directions emphasizing brand value and product design. But 'small matters favorable' reminds us returns are limited—don't overcommit. The top line's 'simple grace, no blame' advises returning to fundamental value rather than chasing flashy concept stocks. In health, fire beneath the mountain symbolizes internal heat—watch for inflammation. Bi also reminds us to attend to external appearance; moderate grooming contributes to psychological well-being.
historicalStoryZh: 贲卦最著名的典故是孔子论"绘事后素"。子夏问孔子"巧笑倩兮，美目盼兮，素以为绚兮"是什么意思，孔子回答"绘事后素"——绘画要先有白色底子，然后才能施加彩色。这正是贲卦"以质为本、文饰为末"的精髓。另一个典故是魏晋时期的"竹林七贤"。嵇康、阮籍等人崇尚自然、不拘礼法，以朴素真率的生活方式对抗当时虚伪的名教礼法，正是上九"白贲"——以不装饰为最高装饰——的历史实践。他们证明了真正的风度不在于外在的华丽，而在于内心的自由。
historicalStoryEn: The most celebrated historical story associated with Bi is Confucius's discussion of 'painting comes after the plain ground.' Zixia asked Confucius the meaning of 'lovely smile with dimples, beautiful eyes so clear, plain silk made gorgeous'—Confucius replied 'painting comes after the plain ground,' meaning painting requires a white base before colors can be applied. This captures the essence of Bi: substance as foundation, adornment as supplement. Another famous story involves the 'Seven Sages of the Bamboo Grove' during the Wei-Jin period. Ji Kang, Ruan Ji, and others championed naturalness and rejected rigid ritual propriety, countering the era's hypocritical Confucian formalism with simple, authentic lifestyles—a historical practice of the top line's 'simple grace': making non-adornment the highest adornment. They proved that true elegance lies not in external splendor but in inner freedom.
relatedHexagramsNote: 贲卦的错卦为困卦（第47卦），文饰之美与困顿之苦形成对比；综卦为噬嗑卦（第21卦），刑罚之实与文饰之美互为表里；互卦为复卦（第24卦），提醒文饰最终要回归质朴本真。

judgmentEn: Splitting Apart. It does not further one to go anywhere.
imageZh: 山附于地，剥；上以厚下安宅。
imageEn: The mountain rests on the earth: the image of Splitting Apart. Thus those above can ensure their position only by giving generously to those below.
interpretationZh: 剥卦象征剥落，山附于地，根基动摇。五阴剥一阳，小人势盛，君子宜静待时机，不宜妄动。
interpretationEn: Bo symbolizes splitting apart and decay. The mountain rests on earth with its base eroding. Five yin lines press upon one yang. The superior man waits quietly.

lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: The leg of the bed is split. Those who persevere are destroyed. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 床腿被剥落，根基受损，坚持不当则凶。
lines.interpretationEn: The leg of the bed is split. The foundation is damaged. Misfortune for those who persist wrongly.
lines.detailZh: 初六阴柔居剥卦之初，是剥落的开始。"剥床以足"描绘床腿被剥蚀——床是人休息的根基，床腿被毁意味着根基开始动摇。"蔑贞凶"表示正道被蔑视，结果是凶险的。初六的剥落看似微小（只是床腿），但这是危险的开端。千里之堤溃于蚁穴，任何大的崩塌都是从微小的裂缝开始的。初六警告我们：当发现根基出现问题时，必须高度警惕，因为这往往是全面崩塌的前兆。
lines.detailEn: The first six, a yin line at the beginning of Bo, marks the start of splitting apart. 'The leg of the bed is split' depicts the bed's legs being eroded—the bed is the foundation of rest, and damaged legs mean the foundation is beginning to shake. 'Contempt for perseverance brings misfortune' indicates that the right way is being scorned, leading to danger. The first six's erosion appears minor (merely the bed legs), but this is the beginning of peril. A thousand-mile levee collapses from an ant hole; every great collapse begins with a tiny crack. The first six warns us: when foundation problems appear, extreme vigilance is essential, for this is often the harbinger of total collapse.

lines.textEn: Six in the second place: The bed is split at the edge. Those who persevere are destroyed. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 床沿被剥落，侵蚀加深，仍不知警惕则凶。
lines.interpretationEn: The bed is split at the edge. Erosion deepens. Misfortune for those who ignore the warning.
lines.detailZh: 六二阴柔居中，剥落继续上升。"剥床以辨"描绘床板的边框被剥蚀——比初六更严重，剥落已经从床腿上升到了床身。"蔑贞凶"同样警告正道被蔑视的凶险。六二虽然居中位，但在剥卦的大势下，中庸之德也无法阻止衰败的蔓延。这说明当大环境恶化时，个人的品德再好也难以独善其身。六二的处境像是一个正直的人在腐败的组织中——虽然自己没有问题，但整体的崩塌不可避免地会波及到自己。
lines.detailEn: The second six, a yin line in the central position, sees the splitting continue upward. 'The edge of the bed is split' depicts the bed frame being eroded—more severe than the first six, the splitting has risen from the legs to the bed itself. 'Contempt for perseverance brings misfortune' again warns of the danger when the right way is scorned. Though the second six occupies the central position, under Bo's prevailing momentum, even the virtue of the mean cannot halt the spread of decay. This demonstrates that when the larger environment deteriorates, even the finest personal virtue cannot ensure one's safety. The second six's situation resembles an upright person in a corrupt organization—though personally blameless, the overall collapse inevitably affects them.

lines.textEn: Six in the third place: He splits with them. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 与剥落者决裂，脱离小人阵营，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: He breaks away from the destructive group. No blame.
lines.detailZh: 六三阴柔居下卦之上，是五个阴爻中唯一与上九正应的。"剥之，无咎"——在剥落的大势中，六三却"无咎"，这是因为它与上九（唯一的阳爻）保持着正应关系。在小人当道的环境中，六三选择与君子（上九）站在一起，虽然身处阴营却心向阳光。六三的智慧在于：即使在最恶劣的环境中，也要保持与正道的联系。"无咎"不是说没有困难，而是说在道义上没有过失。
lines.detailEn: The third six, a yin line atop the lower trigram, is the only one among the five yin lines with a direct correspondence to the top nine. 'Splitting apart—no blame.' Amid the prevailing momentum of splitting, the third six alone incurs 'no blame' because it maintains its correspondence with the top nine (the sole yang line). In an environment dominated by petty people, the third six chooses to stand with the virtuous (the top nine)—though situated among yin, its heart turns toward the light. The wisdom of the third six: even in the most hostile environment, maintain connection with the right path. 'No blame' doesn't mean no difficulty—it means no moral fault.

lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The bed is split up to the skin. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 床面被剥落，灾祸已近身，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: The bed is split to the skin. Disaster is close at hand. Misfortune.
lines.detailZh: 六四阴柔居上卦之初，剥落已经到了最危险的阶段。"剥床以肤"描绘剥落已经触及人的皮肤——不再是剥床了，而是直接伤害到人身。"凶"字直接宣判了结果。六四的剥落已经从物质层面（床）上升到了人身层面（肤），意味着危害已经不可回避。在组织中，这相当于核心团队开始瓦解、关键人才开始流失。到了六四的阶段，已经不是能不能挽救的问题，而是如何减少损失的问题。
lines.detailEn: The fourth six, a yin line at the base of the upper trigram, marks the most dangerous stage of splitting. 'The bed is split up to the skin' depicts the splitting reaching the person's flesh—no longer splitting the bed but directly harming the person. 'Misfortune' directly pronounces the outcome. The fourth six's splitting has escalated from the material level (the bed) to the personal level (the skin), meaning the harm can no longer be avoided. In an organization, this is equivalent to the core team beginning to disintegrate and key talent starting to leave. At the fourth six's stage, the question is no longer whether rescue is possible but how to minimize losses.

lines.textZh: 六五：贯鱼，以宫人宠，无不利。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: A shoal of fishes. Favor comes through the court ladies. Everything acts to further.
lines.interpretationZh: 如鱼贯而入，以宫人之宠得势，顺应时势则无不利。
lines.interpretationEn: A string of fish. Favor through the court ladies. Everything furthers.
lines.detailZh: 六五阴柔居君位，是剥卦中最特殊的一爻。"贯鱼以宫人宠，无不利"描绘用串鱼的方式带领宫人获得恩宠——在危难时刻，六五不是孤军奋战，而是团结身边的人（宫人）形成合力。"贯鱼"象征有序的组织，"宠"象征恩泽和关怀。六五的智慧在于：即使在最不利的环境中，也要善于团结可以团结的力量。六五虽然是阴爻居君位（能力有限），但通过团结内部力量，仍然可以"无不利"。
lines.detailEn: The fifth six, a yin line in the ruler's position, is Bo's most distinctive line. 'A string of fish. Favor comes through the court ladies. Everything acts to further' depicts leading court ladies in an orderly string to receive favor—in times of crisis, the fifth six does not fight alone but unites those nearby (court ladies) to form collective strength. 'A string of fish' symbolizes orderly organization; 'favor' symbolizes grace and care. The wisdom of the fifth six: even in the most unfavorable environment, skillfully unite whatever forces can be united. Though the fifth six is a yin line in the ruler's position (limited capability), by rallying internal forces, it can still achieve 'everything acts to further.'

lines.textZh: 上九：硕果不食，君子得舆，小人剥庐。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: There is a large fruit still uneaten. The superior man receives a carriage. The house of the inferior man is split apart.
lines.interpretationZh: 大果未被吃掉，君子得到车乘，小人则失去居所。一阳犹存，生机未绝。
lines.interpretationEn: A large fruit remains uneaten. The superior man gains a carriage; the inferior man's house splits apart. One yang endures — life persists.
lines.detailZh: 上九阳刚居剥卦之极，是全卦唯一的阳爻，孤悬于五阴之上。"硕果不食"描绘一颗硕大的果实没有被吃掉——在万物凋零的秋天，这颗果实保存了生命的种子。"君子得舆，小人剥庐"区分了两种结局：君子得到车乘（获得支持和出路），小人连房屋都被剥夺。上九是剥卦中最有希望的一爻——虽然处境最危险，但它是复兴的种子。剥尽则复，冬去春来，上九的"硕果"就是来年春天的希望。
lines.detailEn: The top nine, a yang line at the extreme of Bo, is the hexagram's sole yang line, suspended precariously above five yin lines. 'A large fruit still uneaten' depicts a great fruit that has not been consumed—in autumn when all things wither, this fruit preserves the seed of life. 'The superior man receives a carriage; the inferior man's hut is split apart' distinguishes two outcomes: the superior person gains a carriage (receiving support and a way forward), while the petty person loses even their shelter. The top nine is Bo's most hopeful line—though in the most perilous position, it is the seed of revival. When splitting reaches its end, return begins; when winter passes, spring comes. The top nine's 'large fruit' is the hope of next year's spring.

overviewZh: 剥卦是《周易》第二十三卦，由坤下艮上组成，山附于地，象征山体崩塌、剥落于地。"剥"字本义为剥落、剥蚀，引申为衰败、消亡。剥卦五阴一阳，阴气极盛，唯一的阳爻（上九）岌岌可危，象征小人势力膨胀、君子处境艰难。在十二消息卦中，剥卦对应农历九月，秋风萧瑟、万物凋零。《序卦传》云："致饰然后亨则尽矣，故受之以剥。"过度的文饰导致亨通耗尽，于是衰败来临。剥卦的核心智慧在于：衰败是自然规律的一部分，面对衰败要懂得隐忍等待，不可妄动。
overviewEn: Bo (Splitting Apart) is the twenty-third hexagram of the I Ching, composed of Earth (Kun) below and Mountain (Gen) above. The mountain resting on the earth symbolizes the mountain crumbling and splitting apart onto the ground. The character 'Bo' originally means to peel off or erode, extended to mean decline and dissolution. Bo contains five yin lines and only one yang line (the top nine), which stands in peril—symbolizing the expansion of petty forces while the virtuous find themselves in dire straits. In the twelve sovereign hexagram system, Bo corresponds to the ninth lunar month, when autumn winds blow desolately and all things wither. The Sequence of Hexagrams states: 'When adornment has been carried to the highest degree, success is exhausted, hence Bo follows.' Excessive embellishment exhausts prosperity, and decline arrives. The core wisdom of Bo lies in understanding that decline is part of the natural cycle—when facing decline, one must know how to endure patiently and wait, never acting rashly.
judgmentDetailZh: 剥卦卦辞"不利有攸往"极为简洁——不利于前行，不适合采取任何主动行动。这是六十四卦中最消极的卦辞之一，但消极中蕴含着深刻的智慧。当大势已去、阴盛阳衰时，任何主动出击都只会加速灭亡。正确的做法是静待时机、保存实力。剥卦不是教人放弃，而是教人在不利的环境中如何生存。就像冬天的树木落尽叶子，看似死亡，实则是在保存能量等待春天。"不利有攸往"的潜台词是：现在不动，是为了将来更好地行动。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment of Bo—'It does not further one to go anywhere'—is extremely concise: it is unfavorable to proceed, unsuitable for any proactive action. This is one of the most passive judgments among the sixty-four hexagrams, yet within this passivity lies profound wisdom. When the tide has turned and yin overwhelms yang, any aggressive move only accelerates destruction. The correct approach is to wait patiently for the right moment while preserving one's strength. Bo does not teach surrender but teaches how to survive in unfavorable conditions. Like a tree in winter that has shed all its leaves—appearing dead but actually conserving energy to await spring. The subtext of 'it does not further one to go anywhere' is: not moving now is preparation for better action in the future.
imageDetailZh: 象辞"山附于地，剥；上以厚下安宅"蕴含深刻的治理智慧。"山附于地"描绘山体崩塌、附着于地面的景象——山之所以崩塌，是因为根基不牢。"上以厚下安宅"是统治者从中得到的教训：要厚待下层百姓，才能安稳自己的地位（宅）。山的崩塌从底部开始——初六、六二、六三逐层剥落，最终导致上九孤立无援。这告诫领导者：如果不关心基层，基层就会"剥落"，最终整个组织都会崩塌。"厚下"是防止"剥"的根本之道。
imageDetailEn: The Image text—'The mountain rests on the earth: the image of Splitting Apart. Thus those above can ensure their position only by giving generously to those below'—contains profound governance wisdom. 'The mountain resting on the earth' depicts the mountain crumbling onto the ground—the mountain collapses because its foundation is unstable. 'Those above ensure their position by giving generously to those below' is the lesson rulers draw: only by treating the lower classes generously can one secure one's own position. The mountain's collapse begins from the bottom—the first, second, and third lines peel away layer by layer, ultimately leaving the top nine isolated and helpless. This warns leaders: if you neglect the grassroots, the grassroots will 'split apart,' and eventually the entire organization will collapse. 'Giving generously to those below' is the fundamental way to prevent 'splitting apart.'
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】剥卦是最明确的"止损信号"。当行业衰退、公司走下坡路时，不要逆势扩张，要收缩防守、保存实力。六四"剥床以肤"警告衰败已经触及核心利益，必须立即采取行动。六五"贯鱼以宫人宠"暗示在困境中要善于团结内部力量。上九"硕果不食"提醒保留核心种子，为未来复兴做准备。【感情】剥卦象征感情的消磨和冷淡期。不要在这个时候强行挽回，给彼此空间和时间。初六"剥床以足"暗示问题从基础开始动摇。【财运】剥卦是最强烈的"卖出信号"——市场下行趋势已经确立，不要抄底，要果断离场。"不利有攸往"就是不要有任何操作。上九"硕果不食"建议保留现金，等待下一个周期。【健康】剥卦象征身体机能的衰退，要特别注意骨骼和皮肤健康。秋冬季节注意保暖防寒，减少消耗性活动。
modernApplicationEn: In career, Bo is the clearest 'stop-loss signal.' When an industry declines or a company is on a downward trajectory, don't expand against the trend—contract, defend, and preserve strength. The fourth line's 'splitting the bed through to the skin' warns that decline has reached core interests and immediate action is required. The fifth line's 'a string of fish through the favor of court ladies' suggests skillfully uniting internal forces during adversity. The top line's 'a large fruit uneaten' reminds us to preserve core seeds for future revival. In relationships, Bo symbolizes the erosion and cooling period of a relationship. Don't force reconciliation at this time—give each other space and time. The first line's 'splitting the bed by the legs' suggests problems are shaking the foundation. In finance, Bo is the strongest 'sell signal'—the market downtrend is established; don't try to catch the bottom but decisively exit. 'It does not further one to go anywhere' means make no moves at all. The top line's 'large fruit uneaten' advises holding cash and waiting for the next cycle. In health, Bo symbolizes declining bodily functions—pay special attention to bone and skin health. During autumn and winter, stay warm and reduce energy-depleting activities.
historicalStoryZh: 剥卦最著名的典故是商纣王的覆灭。纣王荒淫无道，忠臣比干被剖心、箕子被囚禁、微子出逃——这正是阳爻被逐层剥落的过程。最终武王伐纣，商朝灭亡。纣王的悲剧在于不懂"厚下安宅"，反而横征暴敛、残害忠良，导致根基彻底崩塌。另一个典故是诸葛亮"隆中对"前的隐居。东汉末年天下大乱，诸葛亮深知时机未到（"不利有攸往"），选择在隆中隐居耕读，等待明主。他就像剥卦的上九"硕果不食"——保存自己这颗种子，等待春天的到来。
historicalStoryEn: The most celebrated historical story associated with Bo is the fall of King Zhou of Shang. King Zhou was dissolute and tyrannical—the loyal minister Bi Gan was disemboweled, Ji Zi was imprisoned, and Wei Zi fled. This precisely mirrors the process of yang lines being stripped away layer by layer. Ultimately King Wu conquered Shang, and the dynasty perished. King Zhou's tragedy lay in failing to understand 'giving generously to those below to secure one's position,' instead imposing crushing taxes and persecuting loyal ministers, causing the foundation to collapse completely. Another famous story is Zhuge Liang's seclusion before the 'Longzhong Plan.' During the chaos of the late Eastern Han, Zhuge Liang understood the time was not right ('it does not further one to go anywhere') and chose to live in seclusion at Longzhong, farming and studying while awaiting a worthy lord. He was like Bo's top nine, 'a large fruit uneaten'—preserving himself as a seed, waiting for spring to arrive.
relatedHexagramsNote: 剥卦的错卦为夬卦（第43卦），剥之阴盛与夬之阳决形成对比；综卦为复卦（第24卦），剥复相序，剥尽则复，衰极则兴；互卦为坤卦（第2卦），纯阴之象，提醒剥落过程中阴气极盛。

judgmentZh: 复：亨。出入无疾，朋来无咎。反复其道，七日来复，利有攸往。
judgmentEn: Return. Success. Going out and coming in without error. Friends come without blame. To and fro goes the way. On the seventh day comes return. It furthers one to have somewhere to go.
imageZh: 雷在地中，复；先王以至日闭关，商旅不行，后不省方。
imageEn: Thunder within the earth: the image of the Turning Point. Thus the kings of antiquity closed the passes at the time of solstice. Merchants and strangers did not go about, and the ruler did not travel through the provinces.
interpretationZh: 复卦象征回归，一阳复生于五阴之下，如冬至阳气初回。雷在地中蓄势，万物将复苏，宜休养生息。
interpretationEn: Fu symbolizes return. One yang line reappears beneath five yin lines, like the winter solstice when light returns. Thunder rests within the earth, gathering strength for renewal.

lines.textZh: 初九：不远复，无祗悔，元吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Return from a short distance. No need for remorse. Great good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 迷途不远即返回，不至于后悔，大吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Returning before going far astray. No remorse needed. Great good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 初九是复卦唯一的阳爻，居最下位，是阳气回归的起点。"不远复，无祇悔，元吉"是整部《周易》中最温暖的爻辞之一——走得不远就回来了，不至于深深后悔，大吉大利。"不远复"三个字蕴含着深刻的人生智慧：犯错不可怕，可怕的是在错误的道路上越走越远。及时回头，一切都来得及。孔子最欣赏的弟子颜回被赞为"不贰过"——不犯同样的错误，这正是"不远复"的最佳注脚。
lines.detailEn: The first nine is Fu's sole yang line, occupying the lowest position as the starting point of yang's return. 'Return from a short distance. No need for remorse. Great good fortune' is one of the warmest line texts in the entire I Ching—having not gone far before turning back, there is no cause for deep regret, and great fortune follows. The three characters 'return from a short distance' contain profound life wisdom: making mistakes is not fearsome; what is fearsome is going ever further down the wrong path. Turn back in time, and everything can still be salvaged. Confucius's most admired disciple Yan Hui was praised for 'never repeating a mistake'—the perfect annotation for 'return from a short distance.'

lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Quiet return. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 安然回归，从善如流，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Quiet, graceful return. Following the good. Good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 六二阴柔居中得正，紧邻初九。"休复，吉"的"休"是美好之意——六二的回归是美好的、令人欣慰的。六二之所以能"休复"，是因为它紧邻初九（阳爻），愿意亲近和追随正道。在复卦中，初九是阳气的源头，六二主动靠近这个源头，就像学生主动亲近良师。"吉"字肯定了六二的选择。六二告诉我们：回归正道最好的方式是亲近正直的人，近朱者赤，环境的力量比意志力更可靠。
lines.detailEn: The second six, a yin line achieving centrality and correctness, is adjacent to the first nine. 'Quiet return. Good fortune'—'quiet' means beautiful and admirable. The second six's return is beautiful and gratifying. The second six achieves 'quiet return' because it is adjacent to the first nine (the yang line), willing to approach and follow the right path. In Fu, the first nine is the source of yang energy, and the second six proactively draws near this source, like a student seeking out a good teacher. 'Good fortune' affirms the second six's choice. The second six teaches us: the best way to return to the right path is to associate with upright people—proximity to vermillion makes one red; the power of environment is more reliable than willpower alone.

lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Repeated return. Danger. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 反复回归，虽有危险但无过失，需坚定心志。
lines.interpretationEn: Repeated return. Danger exists but no blame. Strengthen your resolve.
lines.detailZh: 六三阴柔不中不正，居下卦之上。"频复，厉，无咎"描绘反复回归的状态——一次次偏离正道，又一次次回来。"频"字暗示六三的意志不够坚定，总是在正道和歧途之间摇摆。"厉"表示这样做有危险，但"无咎"表示在道义上没有过失——因为至少还在努力回归，比完全放弃好得多。六三的处境像是戒烟反复失败的人——虽然屡戒屡犯，但每次尝试都是值得肯定的。关键是不要放弃尝试。
lines.detailEn: The third six, a yin line achieving neither centrality nor correctness, sits atop the lower trigram. 'Repeated return. Danger. No blame' describes a state of returning again and again—repeatedly straying from the right path, then coming back each time. 'Repeated' implies the third six's resolve is insufficiently firm, constantly wavering between the right path and wrong turns. 'Danger' indicates this is risky, but 'no blame' means there is no moral fault—because at least the effort to return continues, far better than complete abandonment. The third six's situation resembles someone repeatedly failing to quit smoking—though relapsing again and again, each attempt deserves affirmation. The key is never to stop trying.

lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Walking in the midst of others, one returns alone.
lines.interpretationZh: 行于众人之中却独自回归正道，特立独行。
lines.interpretationEn: Walking among others, yet returning alone to the right path.
lines.detailZh: 六四阴柔居上卦之初，处于五阴之中。"中行独复"描绘在众人之中独自回归正道——周围都是阴爻（随波逐流的人），只有六四选择了不同的方向。"中行"表示走在中间（与众人同行），"独复"表示独自回归。这需要极大的勇气和定力——在所有人都走错路的时候，敢于独自转身。六四是复卦中最令人敬佩的一爻，它证明了：真正的回归不需要等待他人，一个人的觉醒就足以改变一切。
lines.detailEn: The fourth six, a yin line at the base of the upper trigram, stands amid five yin lines. 'Walking in the midst of others, one returns alone' depicts returning to the right path alone while surrounded by others—all around are yin lines (people following the crowd), yet only the fourth six chooses a different direction. 'Walking in the midst' means traveling among others; 'returning alone' means turning back independently. This requires tremendous courage and resolve—daring to turn around alone when everyone else is going the wrong way. The fourth six is Fu's most admirable line, proving that true return need not wait for others—one person's awakening is sufficient to change everything.

lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Noblehearted return. No remorse.
lines.interpretationZh: 敦厚地回归正道，无所悔恨。
lines.interpretationEn: Noblehearted return to the good. No remorse.
lines.detailZh: 六五阴柔居君位，与六二正应。"敦复，无悔"的"敦"是敦厚、诚恳之意——六五以最真诚敦厚的态度回归正道。作为君主，六五的回归不仅是个人的觉醒，更是对整个国家方向的纠正。"无悔"表示不会后悔——因为回归正道永远是正确的选择。六五的"敦复"与临卦上六的"敦临"相呼应，都强调以敦厚诚恳的态度面对重大抉择。领导者的回归需要更大的勇气，因为要承认之前的方向是错误的。
lines.detailEn: The fifth six, a yin line in the ruler's position, corresponds with the second six. 'Magnanimous return. No remorse'—'magnanimous' means sincere, honest, and wholehearted. The fifth six returns to the right path with the most genuine and earnest attitude. As the ruler, the fifth six's return is not merely personal awakening but a correction of the entire nation's direction. 'No remorse' means there will be no regret—because returning to the right path is always the correct choice. The fifth six's 'magnanimous return' echoes the top six of Lin's 'magnanimous approach,' both emphasizing facing momentous decisions with sincere earnestness. A leader's return requires even greater courage, for it means acknowledging that the previous direction was wrong.

lines.textZh: 上六：迷复，凶，有灾眚。用行师，终有大败，以其国君，凶；至于十年，不克征。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Missing the return. Misfortune. Misfortune from within and without. If armies are set marching in this way, one will in the end suffer a great defeat, disastrous for the ruler of the country. For ten years it will not be possible to attack again.
lines.interpretationZh: 迷失而不知回归，凶险，有灾祸。若此时用兵，终将大败，十年不能再征。
lines.interpretationEn: Lost and unable to return. Misfortune and disaster. Armies sent forth now will suffer great defeat. Ten years before recovery.
lines.detailZh: 上六阴柔居复卦之极，是回归之道的反面教材。"迷复，凶，有灾眚"——迷失而不知回归，凶险，有天灾人祸。"用行师，终有大败"表示如果在这种迷失状态下出兵作战，最终必然大败。"以其国君凶，至于十年不克征"更是严重警告：连国君都会遭遇凶险，十年之内都无法出征。上六是复卦中唯一的凶爻，它的悲剧在于：所有人都在回归，唯独它迷失不返。机会就在眼前却视而不见，这是最大的悲哀。上六警告我们：错过了回归的时机，代价将是极其惨重的。
lines.detailEn: The top six, a yin line at the extreme of Fu, serves as the cautionary counterexample of the way of return. 'Confused return. Misfortune. Calamities and injuries'—lost and unable to find the way back, bringing danger and both natural and human disasters. 'If armies are used, one will in the end suffer a great defeat' indicates that waging war in this state of confusion inevitably ends in catastrophic defeat. 'For the ruler of the country: misfortune. For ten years it will not be possible to attack' delivers an even graver warning: even the ruler faces danger, unable to mount campaigns for a decade. The top six is Fu's only inauspicious line, and its tragedy lies in this: while everyone else is returning, it alone remains lost. Opportunity stands before it yet goes unseen—this is the greatest sorrow. The top six warns us: missing the moment of return exacts an extraordinarily heavy price.

overviewZh: 复卦是《周易》第二十四卦，由震下坤上组成，地中有雷，象征阳气在地下萌动、即将破土而出。"复"字本义为返回、回复，引申为复兴、恢复。复卦一阳生于五阴之下，是剥卦的综卦——剥尽则复，物极必反。在十二消息卦中，复卦对应冬至（农历十一月），是一年中白昼最短的一天，但从这一天起阳光开始回归。《序卦传》云："物不可以终尽剥，穷上反下，故受之以复。"事物不会永远衰败，到了极点就会反转。复卦的核心智慧在于：黑暗中孕育着光明，衰败的尽头就是复兴的开始。
overviewEn: Fu (Return / The Turning Point) is the twenty-fourth hexagram of the I Ching, composed of Thunder (Zhen) below and Earth (Kun) above. Thunder within the earth symbolizes yang energy stirring underground, about to break through the surface. The character 'Fu' originally means to return or restore, extended to mean revival and recovery. Fu has a single yang line born beneath five yin lines—the inverse hexagram of Bo (Splitting Apart). When splitting reaches its end, return begins; things reverse at their extreme. In the twelve sovereign hexagram system, Fu corresponds to the winter solstice (eleventh lunar month), the shortest day of the year, yet from this day forward sunlight begins its return. The Sequence of Hexagrams states: 'Things cannot be destroyed forever; when exhaustion reaches the top it returns to the bottom, hence Fu follows.' Things will not decline forever—at the extreme point, reversal occurs. The core wisdom of Fu lies in understanding that light gestates within darkness, and the end of decline is the beginning of revival.
judgmentDetailZh: 复卦卦辞"亨，出入无疾，朋来无咎。反复其道，七日来复，利有攸往"充满了希望和力量。"亨"表示复兴之道必然亨通。"出入无疾"表示进出没有障碍和疾病——阳气的回归是自然而然的，不需要强求。"朋来无咎"表示志同道合的朋友会来相助。"反复其道"揭示了宇宙运行的根本规律——阴阳交替、循环往复。"七日来复"指从剥卦（五月卦姤开始阴生）到复卦经历七个阶段，象征一个完整的周期。"利有攸往"与剥卦的"不利有攸往"形成鲜明对比——现在可以行动了！
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment of Fu—'Success. Going out and coming in without error. Friends come without blame. To and fro goes the way. On the seventh day comes return. It furthers one to have somewhere to go'—is filled with hope and power. 'Success' declares that the way of return will surely prosper. 'Going out and coming in without error' means movement encounters no obstacles or illness—the return of yang energy is natural and need not be forced. 'Friends come without blame' means like-minded allies will come to assist. 'To and fro goes the way' reveals the fundamental law of cosmic operation—yin and yang alternate in endless cycles. 'On the seventh day comes return' indicates that from Bo through seven stages to Fu represents a complete cycle. 'It furthers one to have somewhere to go' forms a sharp contrast with Bo's 'it does not further one to go anywhere'—now is the time to act!
imageDetailZh: 象辞"雷在地中，复；先王以至日闭关，商旅不行，后不省方"将自然节律与治国理政完美结合。"雷在地中"描绘冬至时节雷声潜藏于地下的景象——阳气虽然已经开始回归，但仍然微弱，需要呵护。先王效法这一天象，在冬至日采取三项措施："闭关"是关闭城门和关隘，"商旅不行"是停止商业活动和旅行，"后不省方"是君主不巡视四方。这三项措施的目的都是让初生的阳气得到充分的休养和积蓄。这告诉我们：新生事物在萌芽阶段最脆弱，需要保护而非催促。
imageDetailEn: The Image text—'Thunder within the earth: the image of the Turning Point. Thus the kings of antiquity closed the passes at the time of solstice. Merchants and travelers did not go about, and the ruler did not travel through the provinces'—perfectly unites natural rhythm with governance. 'Thunder within the earth' depicts thunder hidden underground at the winter solstice—though yang energy has begun its return, it remains faint and needs nurturing. The ancient kings emulated this pattern with three measures at the solstice: 'closing the passes' means shutting city gates and mountain passes; 'merchants and travelers did not go about' means halting commerce and travel; 'the ruler did not travel' means the sovereign ceased provincial tours. All three measures serve one purpose: allowing the newborn yang energy sufficient rest and accumulation. This teaches us: new things are most fragile in their germinal stage, requiring protection rather than pressure.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】复卦是最强的"抄底信号"和"重新出发"的标志。经历了低谷之后，转机已经出现。初九"不远复"鼓励及时回归正道，不要在错误的路上走太远。但"七日来复"提醒复兴需要时间，不能急于求成。六四"中行独复"赞扬在众人迷失时独自回归正道的勇气。【感情】复卦象征感情的修复和重新开始。经历了冷淡期或分手后，如果双方都有诚意，关系可以修复。初九"不远复"建议趁误会还不深时及时沟通。六三"频复"警告反复无常会损害信任。【财运】复卦是经典的"底部反转"信号。市场经历了充分的下跌后，阳气初生，可以开始逐步建仓。但要注意"雷在地中"——反弹初期力量微弱，不宜重仓，要循序渐进。【健康】复卦对应冬至养生，是一年中最适合进补和休养的时节。"闭关"提醒要充分休息，"不远复"鼓励生病后及时调养、尽早恢复。
modernApplicationEn: In career, Fu is the strongest 'bottom-fishing signal' and marker of 'fresh starts.' After experiencing a trough, the turning point has appeared. The first line's 'return from a short distance' encourages promptly returning to the right path before straying too far. But 'on the seventh day comes return' reminds us that revival takes time—don't rush. The fourth line's 'walking in the midst of others, one returns alone' praises the courage to return to the right path when everyone else is lost. In relationships, Fu symbolizes repair and new beginnings. After a cooling period or breakup, if both parties are sincere, the relationship can be restored. The first line's 'return from a short distance' suggests communicating before misunderstandings deepen. The third line's 'repeated return' warns that inconsistency damages trust. In finance, Fu is the classic 'bottom reversal' signal. After the market has fully declined, yang energy emerges and one can begin gradually building positions. But note 'thunder within the earth'—early-stage rebounds are weak, so avoid heavy positions and proceed gradually. In health, Fu corresponds to winter solstice wellness, the year's most suitable time for nourishment and rest. 'Closing the passes' reminds us to rest fully; 'return from a short distance' encourages prompt recuperation after illness for early recovery.
historicalStoryZh: 复卦最著名的典故是越王勾践"卧薪尝胆"。勾践被吴王夫差打败后，忍辱负重、卧薪尝胆二十年，最终灭吴复国。勾践的经历完美诠释了复卦的精神：在最低谷时不放弃希望，默默积蓄力量，等待时机成熟后一举翻盘。"七日来复"在勾践身上变成了"二十年来复"，但核心道理相同——只要种子还在，春天终会到来。另一个典故是周文王被囚羑里。文王被商纣王囚禁七年，在狱中推演八卦、著述《周易》，将苦难转化为智慧。出狱后他积蓄力量，最终为武王伐纣奠定了基础。
historicalStoryEn: The most celebrated historical story associated with Fu is King Goujian of Yue's 'sleeping on brushwood and tasting gall.' After being defeated by King Fuchai of Wu, Goujian endured humiliation for twenty years, sleeping on rough brushwood and tasting bitter gall daily to maintain his resolve, ultimately destroying Wu and restoring his kingdom. Goujian's experience perfectly embodies Fu's spirit: never abandoning hope at the lowest point, silently accumulating strength, and seizing the moment for a complete reversal when the time is ripe. 'On the seventh day comes return' became 'in twenty years comes return' for Goujian, but the core truth is identical—as long as the seed survives, spring will eventually arrive. Another famous story is King Wen of Zhou's imprisonment at Youli. King Wen was imprisoned by King Zhou of Shang for seven years, during which he developed the hexagram system and composed the I Ching, transforming suffering into wisdom. After his release, he accumulated strength, ultimately laying the foundation for King Wu's conquest of Shang.
relatedHexagramsNote: 复卦的错卦为姤卦（第44卦），复之阳生与姤之阴生形成对比；综卦为剥卦（第23卦），剥复相序，剥尽则复，体现物极必反的规律；互卦为坤卦（第2卦），纯阴之中孕育一阳，象征希望。
